extraction.article_title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access

ttps.description: Escape to Host via container runtime abuse and privileged container creation after authorization bypass.
Exploit public-facing application/API weakness by abusing Docker Engine API request handling and authz plugin behavior.

attack_chain: Attacker obtains access to Docker Engine API protected by an authorization plugin.
Attacker submits an oversized container-creation request padded beyond the body-handling threshold.
Docker daemon forwards the request to the authz plugin without the request body.
Authz plugin cannot inspect the body and allows the request.
Docker daemon processes the full request and creates a privileged container.
Container gains access to host filesystem and sensitive host data.

proposed_vectors.name_en: Oversized Docker API body truncation to bypass AuthZ inspection
proposed_vectors.name_ko: Docker API 본문 초과로 인한 AuthZ 검사 우회

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: Abuse a size-dependent request handling flaw where the authorization layer receives no request body when the HTTP payload exceeds a threshold, allowing a privileged action to be approved based on incomplete context.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
POST /v1.45/containers/create HTTP/1.1
Host: docker.example:2375
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 1500123

{"Image":"alpine","Cmd":["sh","-c","id > /host/tmp/pwned"],"HostConfig":{"Privileged":true,"Binds":["/:/host:rw"]},"Labels":{"pad":""}}
detect: HTTP 201 Created despite authz policy that normally blocks privileged containers
Container starts with Privileged=true and host bind mount present
Evidence of host file write such as /host/tmp/pwned
AuthZ logs show request allowed with missing or empty body context
